("security: wild-audit — {} block(s), {} justified, {} unjustified" , audit . inventory . len () , audit . justified () , audit . unjustified ()) ; if explain { print ! ("{}" , explain_wild (& audit)) ; } else { for f in & audit . findings { println ! ("  [{}] {} at {}: {}" , f . severity . as_str () , f . rule , f . at , f . why) ; } } if audit . findings . iter () . any (| f | f . severity >= Severity :: High) { failing = true ; } if strict && ! audit . findings . is_empty () { failing = true ; } if do_secrets { match run_script (& dir , "scripts/checks/secrets.sh" , "secrets") { Coverage :: Ok => println ! ("security: supply-chain — FAIL (advisory/policy finding)") ; failing = true ; } } } let coverage = if reduced { "REDUCED" } else { "FULL" } ; if failing { eprintln ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Orchestrate an existing check script, classifying its result honestly (skip ≠ pass). The scripts exit"] # [doc = " 0 on success *or* graceful skip and print an `ok`/`skip`/`FAIL` marker — so exit code alone cannot tell"] # [doc = " \"clean\" from \"scanner absent\". ("{}{}" , String :: from_utf8_lossy (& output . stdout) , String :: from_utf8_lossy (& output . stderr)) ; if text . contains ("FAIL") { Coverage :: Fail } else if text . contains ("skip") { Coverage :: Reduced } else { Coverage :: Ok } }

Emitting bare `len(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Collect every `.myc` under `dir` (recursively, sorted); skipping hidden entries and `target/`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns an I/O error string if a directory cannot be read."] pub fn collect_myc (dir : & Path) -> Result < Vec < PathBuf > , String > { let mut out = Vec :: new () ; walk (dir , & mut out) ? ; out . sort () ; Ok (out) }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `Vec :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: fn walk (dir : & Path , out : & mut Vec < PathBuf >) -> Result < () , String > { let entries = std :: fs :: read_dir (dir) . map_err (| e | format ! ("{}: {e}" , dir . display ())) ? ; let mut paths : Vec < PathBuf > = entries . filter_map (| e | e . ok () . map (| e | e . path ())) . collect () ; paths . sort () ; for path in paths { let name = path . file_name () . and_then (| s | s . to_str ()) . unwrap_or_default () ; if name . starts_with ('.') || name == "target" { continue ; } if path . is_dir () { walk (& path , out) ? ; } else if path . extension () . is_some_and (| x | x == "myc") { out . push (path) ; } } Ok (()) }
gaps.reason: unsupported expression form `std :: fs :: read_dir (dir) . map_err (| e | format ! ("{}: {e}" , dir . display ())) ?`
